channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: Microsoft says Russia trying to smear Harris with deepfake video, AI
item.description: Russia is attempting to “undermine” Vice President Harris’s campaign using artificial intelligence (AI) generated videos while China has begun targeting a series of GOP congressional candidates ahead of November, according to a new report from Microsoft.
item.dc:creator: Miranda Nazzaro, The Hill

item.title: Gartner: 13 AI insights for enterprise IT
item.description: Artificial intelligence is an early stage technology and the hype around it is palpable, but IT leaders need to take many challenges into consideration before making major commitments for their enterprises.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Qualcomm’s license battle with Arm puts many AI-enabled Copilot+ PCs in peril
item.description: As its long-running dispute with Arm turned into a war of words this week, the stakes for chip giant Qualcomm and its technology partners, including Microsoft, couldn’t be higher. Along with MediaTek and Apple, Qualcomm is one of the biggest suppliers of chips for use in smartphones and tablets.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Want your server to access more than 100,000 DIMM slots in one go? This Korean startup claims that is CXL 3.1-based technology can help you scale to more than 100PB of RAM — but it will cost nearly $5 billion
item.description: The possibilities are endless, but the costs might be a bridge too far for even the largest enterprises.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: How Anthropic’s new ‘computer use’ ability could further AI automation
item.description: Anthropic’s Claude 3.5 Sonnet large language model has gained a new ability: operating a computer. The new ability, which the company is calling “computer use,” is currently in beta test.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: 50x faster, 50x thriftier: UK AI startup backed by Arm delivers stunning gains in performance and power consumption using a cheap $30 system board
item.description: AI startup wants to make GPU training obsolete; benchmarks on $30 development board show stunning results.
item.dc:creator: waynewilliams@onmail.com (Wayne Williams), TechRadar

item.title: DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity
item.description: AI is one of the most potent tools supplementing the cybersecurity efforts of federal agencies, but DHS is ensuring it's implemented responsibly and securely. The post DHS fosters intentional approach to AI for cybersecurity first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Daisy Thornton, Federal News Network

item.title: Fox News AI Newsletter: 'Wicked' star Ariana Grande's gripe with AI
item.description: Stay up to date on the latest AI technology advancements and learn about the challenges and opportunities AI presents now and for the future.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: As coal plants shut in Romania, some miners transition to green energy while others are reluctant
item.description: PETRILA, Romania (AP) — For many years, Sebastian Tirinticǎ worked in a coal mine, just like his father and grandfather before him.
item.dc:creator: ANCA GURZU, Cipher News, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: You’re going to have a robot coworker sooner than you think
item.description: They marched in line, interacted with guests, and poured drinks while making small talk. The vision of the future Elon Musk put forward with his Optimus humanoid robots at Tesla’s recent Cybercab event earlier this month was as astounding as it was unbelievable.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: Kamala Harris is going all in on Snapchat. But Donald Trump is still banned
item.description: Since July, Kamala Harris’s campaign has purchased more than 5,000 Snapchat ads, amounting to an estimated $​​5.3 million. It’s not hard to understand why: Snapchat is a haven for young people.
item.dc:creator: Henry Chandonnet, Fast Company

item.title: Gartner: Top 10 strategic technology trends for 2025
item.description: Gartner is sharing its annual look at the hot technologies enterprise customers should be watching out for in the coming years.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: One of the world’s largest book publishers adds AI warnings to its books
item.description: To make generative AI tools (genAI) work as well as possible, tech companies have chosen to train their large language models (LLMs) on large amounts of text, even though doing so could run afoul of copyright laws.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Biden administration to provide $325 million for new Michigan semiconductor factory
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — The Biden administration said Monday that it would provide up to $325 million to Hemlock Semiconductor for a new factory, a move that could help give Democrats a political edge in the swing state of Michigan ahead of election day.
item.dc:creator: JOSH BOAK, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Slack researcher discusses the fear, loathing and excitement surrounding AI in the workplace
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — Artificial intelligence's recent rise to the forefront of business has left most office workers wondering how often they should use the technology and whether a computer will eventually replace them.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: AI needs Apple, which is why time is on its side
item.description: Apple was behind on smartphones, until it wasn’t. It lagged the crowd in digital music players, until it didn’t. There was a time when it was woeful on wearables, until it that changed.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: How Kubecost shines a light on GPU efficiency
item.description: Many enterprise engineering teams—perhaps most at this point—have dived headlong into GPUs to build out proofs-of-concept and operationalize new products. While competitive pressures dictated the direction, GPU cost concerns have begun moving from the back burner to the front.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Stanford researchers map ‘White-Only’ properties using AI
item.description: Racially restrictive covenants are clauses in property deeds that ban individuals of a particular race from buying a home.
item.dc:creator: Hamza Fahmy, NewsNation

item.title: Rising crypto scams leave Americans reeling from billions in losses
item.description: Scammers have illustrated that cryptocurrency is not as foolproof as it has promised. Americans lost $5.6 billion to crypto scams in 2023, according to the FBI.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month. Here's how to stay safe from scams
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — October is Cybersecurity Awareness Month, which means it's the perfect time to learn how to protect yourself from scams. “Scams have become so sophisticated now.
item.dc:creator: ADRIANA MORGA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Chinese humanoid robot could be the future of affordable in-home care
item.description: A humanoid robot made by a Chinese company proposes to redefine personalized elderly care and assistance for people with disabilities.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: US to probe Tesla's 'Full Self-Driving' system after pedestrian killed in low visibility conditions
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — The U.S. government's road safety agency is investigating Tesla's “Full Self-Driving” system after getting reports of crashes in low-visibility conditions, including one that killed a pedestrian.
item.dc:creator: TOM KRISHER, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Judge delays order in antitrust case requiring Google to open up its app store
item.description: SAN FRANCISCO (AP) — A federal judge on Friday delayed an order requiring Google to open up its Android app store to more competition until an appeals court decides whether to block the shake-up because of legal questions surrounding a jury's verdict that branded Google as an illegal monopolist.
item.dc:creator: MICHAEL LIEDTKE, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: 6 key mobile and IoT/OT attack trend findings
item.description: The role of the CISO continues to expand, driven by the rising number of breaches and cyberattacks like ransomware, as well as SEC requirements for public organizations to disclose material breaches.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Trump family will get 75% of crypto token revenue
item.description: The Trump family's cryptocurrency platform, World Liberty Financial, indicated in a report released Thursday that DT Marks DEFI LLC, a Delaware-based company connected to the former president, could get 75 percent of WLF's protocol revenues.
item.dc:creator: Juliann Ventura, NewsNation

item.title: Data center provider fakes Tier 4 data center certificate to bag $11M SEC deal
item.description: Deepak Jain, CEO of a Maryland-based IT services firm, has been indicted for fraud and making false statements after allegedly falsifying a Tier 4 data center certification to secure a $10.7 million contract with the US Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: This gigayacht has a glass-bottom pool and stunning underwater lounge
item.description: An architecture firm has unveiled the concept for a 454-foot gigayacht that combines luxury, innovation and style and can accommodate 22 guests and 52 crew members.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Alabama man arrested in SEC social media account hack that led the price of bitcoin to spike
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — An Alabama man was arrested Thursday for his alleged role in the January hack of a U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission social media account that led the price of bitcoin to spike, the Justice Department said. Eric Council Jr.
item.dc:creator: ALANNA DURKIN RICHER, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: HPE, Dell launch another round of AI servers
item.description: The AI spin continues, with Hewlett Packard Enterprise and Dell Technologies both introducing new servers oriented towards training large language models (LLM).
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Gluware expands network automation platform with AI copilots, GitHub integration
item.description: Gluware today announced a series of upcoming features for its network automation platform, designed to streamline network management and reduce downtime across diverse network environments.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: Alabama man arrested for hacking Securities and Exchange Commission X account
item.description: Following the unauthorized announcement made during the hack, the DOJ said the price of bitcoin increased by more than $1,000 per bitcoin.
item.dc:creator: Logan Sparkman, NewsNation

item.title: Hit the lights: Mets outfit Citi Field for electric light shows
item.description: NEW YORK (AP) — The Mets are lighting up Citi Field, even before their batters step up to the plate. About 70 front-office staffers scattered around the ballpark for over five hours to install 3,500 strips of LED lights on each railing ahead of New York's first home postseason game.
item.dc:creator: RONALD BLUM, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News
